channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fetterman was elected to challenge convention. Now, he's challenging his fellow Democrats
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — When Democrat John Fetterman got elected to Pennsylvania's U.S. Senate seat, many backers hoped he'd challenge convention and the status quo. He did and has — just not in the way many had expected.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kristi Noem, Trump's homeland security pick, faces scrutiny on immigration plans
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem is set to face senators during her confirmation hearing as she vies to become the next homeland security secretary.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana And Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Border app that became 'a salvation' for migrants to legally enter the US may end
item.description: The CBP One app has brought nearly 1 million people to the U.S. on two-year permits with eligibility to work since January 2023, but it could end under President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden thanks troops for ‘strength' and ‘integrity' in unprecedented times at farewell address to military
item.description: President Biden on Thursday thanked U.S. service members and defended his military policies during his commander in chief farewell address.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams to meet President-elect Trump in Florida meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams will be meeting with President-elect Donald Trump on Friday, a spokesperson for the mayor has confirmed. Adams is traveling to Palm Beach, Florida, to discuss the New Yorker’s priorities.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, PIX 11

item.title: Chinese President Xi Jinping will send a special representative to Trump's inauguration
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping will not attend President-elect Donald Trump’s swearing-in ceremony, but he is sending Vice President Han Zheng as his special representative.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: O'Reilly: Trump's past mob dealings prepared him to handle Putin
item.description: Bill O'Reilly thinks Trump's willingness to talk with anyone, even the mob, will serve him well when dealing with Putin.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moody announces Senate plans, DeSantis names AG replacement
item.description: After weeks of speculation, Gov. Ron DeSantis appointed Ashley Moody to replace Marco Rubio, who plans to leave his senate seat.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump offered a bountiful batch of campaign promises that come due on Day 1
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After Donald Trump becomes president again on Monday, he is on the hook for achieving a hefty chunk of his promises even before the day is out. One of those promises is to make you dizzy. “Your head will spin when you see what’s going to happen," he said of Day 1.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ releases final violent crime numbers for Biden administration
item.description: The Department of Justice this week released a final report on violent crime statistics for the Biden administration as President-elect Trump prepares to take office next week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas judge says states can revive challenge to abortion pill access nationwide
item.description: A federal judge in Texas is allowing three other states to pursue a challenge seeking to restrict access to the abortion pill mifepristone nationwide.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Court aims for fall trial date in sexual misconduct lawsuit against Fletcher
item.description: The lawsuit facing Nathan Fletcher over allegations of sexual misconduct could move to a civil trial as soon as fall 2025, as both sides continue to bump heads over text messages between the ex-supervisor's accuser and a friend.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Feds refer Texas’ Harvey funding discrimination case to Justice Department
item.description: The U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development confirmed its finding that Texas unfairly favored white residents in rural areas when it distributed $1 billion in Harvey aid in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Zach Despart, KPRC 2

item.title: Ellison joins 13 states to defend health care coverage for DACA recipients
item.description: The effort is in response to a legal challenge led by Kansas and several other states against a Biden administration rule.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Johnson appoints new Intelligence chairman after surprise shakeup
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson appointed Rep. Rick Crawford, R-Ark., as the new chairman of the House Intelligence Committee on Thursday after asserting that the panel needed a fresh start.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's EPA nominee stays vague on plans as senators press him on climate views
item.description: Lee Zeldin said Americans deserve a clean environment “without suffocating the economy” during his Senate confirmation hearing Thursday to lead the Environmental Protection Agency, a department likely to play a central role in President-elect Donald Trump’s pledge to slash federal regulations and pr
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turner tackles HUD program reforms at confirmation hearing for housing secretary
item.description: There were sports analogies galore but few policy specifics during Scott Turner ’s confirmation hearing on Thursday to become housing secretary.
item.dc:creator: SALLY HO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI closed DEI office in December, agency says
item.description: The FBI confirmed to Fox News Digital that the agency closed its office of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in December.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken defends US policy on Gaza as his final State Department briefing is interrupted by protests
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Outgoing Secretary of State Antony Blinken on Thursday defended Biden administration policies on Israel’s war with Hamas after a ceasefire agreement in Gaza was reached, facing protests that interrupted his final news conference at the State Department.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New US sanctions target Sudan's warring military leader
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. imposed sanctions on the leader of Sudan's military Thursday, saying he was blocking humanitarian aid and bombing hospitals, schools and markets in a war against an armed rival that has created a widening famine and the world's largest displacement crisis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson names Rick Crawford to replace Mike Turner atop Intel Committee
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Thursday selected Rep. Rick Crawford (R-Ark.) to chair the House Intelligence Committee, elevating the veteran panel member to the role after he removed Rep. Mike Turner (R-Ohio) from the top job.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than a dozen Democratic AGs say they'll defend gun regulations ahead of Trump's 2nd term
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — More than a dozen Democratic state attorneys general said Thursday they plan to defend two gun regulations now being challenged in court, including one banning devices that enable semiautomatic guns to fire more quickly.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thompson, chair of Jan. 6 committee, has talked with White House about pardons
item.description: Rep. Bennie Thompson (D-Miss.), who headed Congress’s special investigation into the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, said Tuesday that he has been in talks with the White House about presidential pardons.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Florida's Rubio and Bondi face confirmation hearings
item.description: Former state attorney general and Tampa native Pam Bondi and Senator Marco Rubio are set to appear on Capitol Hill for confirmation hearings, Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: U.S. attorneys for Michigan resign before Trump takes office
item.description: Both of Michigan's U.S. attorneys have announced they will resign from their posts before Donald Trump is sworn into office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, NewsNation

item.title: These 2 Democrats voted for House Republicans' bill to bar transgender athletes from girls sports
item.description: Reps. Vicente Gonzalez and Henry Cuellar, both of Texas, were the only Democrats to side with Republicans on Tuesday to pass legislation meant to bar transgender athletes from competing in girls and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin’s governor creates new violence-prevention office in wake of school shooting
item.description: Wisconsin’s Democratic governor on Tuesday created a new office dedicated to preventing gun violence, a month after a school shooting not far from the state Capitol and an idea that drew immediate opposition from Republicans who said it was misguided.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Michelle Obama's absence was notable given her tough past criticism of Trump, who succeeded her husband in office.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth confronts allegations of misconduct as senators grill Trump’s choice for Pentagon chief
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth was combative at times, as he was forced to confront the allegations of misconduct and his own comments that are far from the military mainstream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge: DFL candidate’s election stands for Shakopee-area Minnesota House seat
item.description: State Rep. Brad Tabke, a DFL Minnesota House incumbent who narrowly won reelection, should be able to begin a new term, a judge ruled Tuesday — despite a GOP election challenge centering on a pool of ballots that were cast but never counted.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota House DFLers boycott opening session; Republicans elect their own speaker
item.description: DFLers on Tuesday made good on their threat to keep the Minnesota House in limbo as the parties fight over control. House Republicans pushed ahead anyway in the half-empty chamber. It’s all likely headed to the courts.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Senate District 60 special election
item.description: Minnesota Senate District 60 is holding a special election to fill the vacancy left by DFL Sen. Kari Dziedzic who died last month.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ACLU sues Gov. DeSantis on behalf of Florida voters over special elections
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union is suing Gov. Ron DeSantis for not calling special elections for state House and Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
